comment_text.sn3.4:1.3: Here again we see a progress in their relationship. Now Paseandi is reflecting on the Dhamma and bringing his own thoughts to the Buddha. Even though Pasenadi is not said to have attained any of the noble paths, this Sutta reveals an insightful grasp of human psychology.
comment_text.sn3.4:1.19: Action is paramount because it is the cause of feelings and emotions. The path forward starts with recognizing that it is within your power to change how you treat yourself.
comment_text.sn3.4:4.1: The “terminator” (antaka) is death, sometimes personified as Māra (eg. SN 4.1:6.4).
comment_text.sn3.4:4.6: This simile also employed at Dhp 2, Thag 17.3:28, and MN 91:8.4.
comment_text.sn3.4:5.3: That deeds (kamma) are one’s own (saka) is a stock reflection (eg. MN 135:4.1, AN 5.57:1.7).
comment_text.sn3.4:6.2: Nicaya (“investing”) is more literally, “piling up”.

root_text.sn3.4:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.4:1.3: “idha mayhaṁ, bhante, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn3.4:1.4: ‘kesaṁ nu kho piyo attā, kesaṁ appiyo attā’ti?
root_text.sn3.4:1.6: ‘ye ca kho keci kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.4:1.9: “piyo no attā”ti, atha kho tesaṁ appiyo attā’.
root_text.sn3.4:1.11: Yañhi appiyo appiyassa kareyya, taṁ te attanāva attano karonti;
root_text.sn3.4:1.13: Ye ca kho keci kāyena sucaritaṁ caranti, vācāya sucaritaṁ caranti, manasā sucaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.4:1.16: ‘appiyo no attā’ti; atha kho tesaṁ piyo attā.
root_text.sn3.4:1.18: Yañhi piyo piyassa kareyya, taṁ te attanāva attano karonti;
root_text.sn3.4:2.2: Ye hi keci, mahārāja, kāyena duccaritaṁ caranti, vācāya duccaritaṁ caranti, manasā duccaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.4:2.5: ‘piyo no attā’ti, atha kho tesaṁ appiyo attā.
root_text.sn3.4:2.7: Yañhi, mahārāja, appiyo appiyassa kareyya, taṁ te attanāva attano karonti;
root_text.sn3.4:2.9: Ye ca kho keci, mahārāja, kāyena sucaritaṁ caranti, vācāya sucaritaṁ caranti, manasā sucaritaṁ caranti;
root_text.sn3.4:2.15: Yañhi, mahārāja, piyo piyassa kareyya, taṁ te attanāva attano karonti;

translation_text.sn3.4:1.2: Seated to one side, King Pasenadi said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn3.4:1.3: “Just now, sir, as I was in private retreat this thought came to mind.
translation_text.sn3.4:1.4: ‘Who are those who love themselves? And who are those who don’t love themselves?’
translation_text.sn3.4:1.6: ‘Those who do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind
translation_text.sn3.4:1.11: It’s because they treat themselves like an enemy.
translation_text.sn3.4:1.12: That’s why they don’t love themselves.
translation_text.sn3.4:1.13: Those who do good things by way of body, speech, and mind
translation_text.sn3.4:1.16: “I don’t love myself”, they do really.
translation_text.sn3.4:1.18: It’s because they treat themselves like a loved one.
translation_text.sn3.4:1.19: That’s why they do love themselves.’”
translation_text.sn3.4:2.1: “That’s so true, great king! That’s so true!” said the Buddha. And he repeated the king’s statement, adding:
translation_text.sn3.4:3.1: “If you knew your self as beloved,
translation_text.sn3.4:3.2: you’d not yoke yourself to wickedness.
translation_text.sn3.4:3.3: For happiness is not easy to find
translation_text.sn3.4:3.4: by someone who does bad deeds.
translation_text.sn3.4:4.1: When you’re seized by the terminator
translation_text.sn3.4:4.2: as you give up your human life,
translation_text.sn3.4:4.3: what can you call your own?
translation_text.sn3.4:4.4: What do you take when you go?
translation_text.sn3.4:4.6: like a shadow that never leaves?
translation_text.sn3.4:5.1: Both the good and the bad
translation_text.sn3.4:5.2: that a mortal does in this life
translation_text.sn3.4:5.3: is what they can call their own.
translation_text.sn3.4:5.4: That’s what they take when they go.
translation_text.sn3.4:5.6: like a shadow that never leaves.
translation_text.sn3.4:6.1: That’s why you should do good,
translation_text.sn3.4:6.3: The good deeds of sentient beings
translation_text.sn3.4:6.4: support them in the next world.”

variant_text.sn3.4:5.5: Taṁvassa → tañcassa (bj, sya-all, km, pts2ed); taṁ cassa (pts1ed)

reference_text.sn3.4:1.1: bj13.136, cck15.98, dr15.98, ms12S1_596, msdiv115, pts-vp-pli1ed1.72, pts-vp-pli2ed1.165, sya15.103, vri23.88
